programme.desc: Married parents Tom and Kirsty check in to fulfil Toms fantasy of experimenting with another man. Natalie and Tom explore a threesome with Precious, insecurities could get in the way.

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George attempts to bring the grandeur back to a Georgian town house in Cheshire thats been stripped of its original features, and tackles a 380-year-old Jacobean cottage.

programme.title: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programme.desc: Janice says goodbye to some of her beloved sheep, Matts makeover of the patio and seating area begins to take shape, and the goats venture to pastures new.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: In Spain, Jo and Ken fling open their doors to the flamboyance of flamenco. In France, Jon and Angela turn a shed into a wedding venue, with themselves as guinea pigs.

programme.title: Jasmine Harmans Renovation in the Sun
programme.desc: As the enormity of the build sets in, work gets underway to make the place liveable before the family move in. And Jasmine visits a friend for some renovation inspiration.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sonal and Tina cant wait to swap busy Birmingham for a new home on Waless Gower Peninsula and a bolthole on the island of Corfu. Can Jonnie Irwin and Laura Hamilton deliver their dream?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sue and Tony want a holiday bolthole in the French city of Carcassonne. With a property already on their radar, can any of Craig Rowes options for their £100k win them over?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Northamptonshire, makeup artist Jules Osmany hopes that her Indian/ Moroccan fusion menu and laid-back attitude to life will win her the prize.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Second up in Northamptonshire is funeral service manager Peter Pickering, who is dead set on hosting a traditional dinner party reminiscent of the 1920s. However, his guests dont like the sound of his bread and butter pudding.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Stay-at-home mum Stephanie Reeders nerves are evident from the outset on her night in Northamptonshire. Shes planning to serve classic fish and chips while offering a glamorous swishing session for fun.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Tweny-six-year-old business planning manager Lauren Oxlade is the penultimate host in Northamptonshire. She is an organised and passionate cook and has a few surprising friends who shes invited along to her party.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Last to try his hand in Northamptonshire is managing director and proud northerner Carl Johnson, with a menu that includes gammon cooked in two litres of cola.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It: Brilliant Builds
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at some of their most resplendent renovations, and deliver handy tips and tricks to help us maximise the spend on our precious bricks and mortar.

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George helps a couple whose Weymouth home was destroyed overnight by a flash flood, and revisits a couple in Chiswick to find out whats next on their demolition list.

programme.title: Batch from Scratch: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: In Nottingham, Emma and Matts hectic schedules mixed with two energetic kids is a recipe for mealtime mayhem. Can they turn chaotic convenience into bountiful batching?

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Scooby the Great Dane seeks a new recruit for the gang. Its a double date for the Davies family with a Labrador and German Shepherd. And is Fox the Pomeranian about to get lucky?

programme.title: Bill Baileys Vietnam
programme.desc: In stunning Sa Pa, comedian Bill Bailey uncovers longstanding cultural traditions - alongside the healing properties of a bath.

programme.title: Bill Bailey: Qualmpeddler
programme.desc: Bill Baileys hilarious live show, recorded at the Hammersmith Apollo. Bill takes on the coalition, popular culture, social media, celebrity and cheap shark-diving.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Cops
programme.desc: After leading police on a slow pursuit, a sharply dressed driver has a heated talk with another deputy; despite her tears, police unravel a womans lies one-by-one; a traditionally bad omen turns into good luck for a couple.

programme.desc: Sailing instructor Kaye Morgan (Pamela Bach) is reunited with her old boyfriend, pro surfer Wes Masters (John Bradley); Stephanie (Alexandra Paul) has cancer surgery; Neely (Gena Lee Nolin) comes between Caroline and Logan (Yasmine Bleeth, Jaason Simmons). With David Hasselhoff.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: While diving for buried treasure, Cody and Stephanie have a dangerous underwater encounter with drug runners.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Reality imitates art when aspiring filmmaker Logan Fowler attempts to shoot a "Jaws" tribute and real sharks start attacking swimmers.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch gets into trouble while judging a beauty contest; Caroline enrolls in an acting class and falls for the teacher.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: C.J. befriends an eccentric beach dweller who thanks her with a generous gift; a rebellious Hobie takes a lifeguard trainee out for some fun.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch becomes stranded on an uninhabited island with a socialite who "bought" him in a bachelor auction; Cody joins a roller hockey team.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: An extremely hot day brings lots of people and problems to the beach, including an overzealous Secret Service agent planning a visit by the president.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch helps Special Olympics athletes raise money by organizing a beach competition featuring MTV personalities.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: C.J.s mother arrives from Las Vegas with the mob hot on her trail in pursuit of a computer disk containing details about her gangster boyfriends gambling profits.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch (David Hasselhoff) and the Baywatch crew face tough rivals from Australia in a lifeguard competition.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Donnas (Donna DErrico) appearance in Playboy causes problems for Mitch (David Hasselhoff); an earthquake traps a junior lifeguard.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Some of the Baywatch lifeguards try out for a new search and rescue unit after they help firefighters battle a blaze on a pier.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: A pollution problem in the bay hits home when Mitch becomes deathly ill during a practice swim for an Ironman competition.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch has a busy day rescuing an injured swimmer and dealing with an editor who wants to feature him in her magazine; Stephanie and Toms wedding gets moved up.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch and Neely join newlyweds Stephanie and Tom on a schooner for an adventure cruise that turns tragic when a hurricane hits.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch acquires some unsolicited fame when he rescues a TV talk show host; Caroline and C.J. adopt an orphaned dog that exhibits uncanny behavior.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Caroline (Yasmine Bleeth) is assisted on a rescue by a mysterious boy; Neelys (Gena Lee Nolin) ex-husband makes a surprise visit.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch and Samantha (David Hasselhoff, Nancy Valen) reach a turning point in their relationship; Mannys mother (Vikki Carr) visits. With Jose Solano.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch and Caroline rescue teenage lovers who attempt to commit suicide; Cody and C.J. encounter what they believe to be a mermaid.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: While out at sea testing new diving equipment, Sam, Neely and Caroline (Nancy Valen, Gena Lee Nolin, Yasmine Bleeth) rescue the pilot (Wiley Pickett) of a downed plane who is transporting disease organisms belonging to a terrorist. With David Hasselhoff.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Caroline must relive the guilt of a failed rescue when she is sued by the victims mother; C.J. considers motherhood but doesnt think Cody is ready.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: C.J. forms a bond with an injured sea lion and a water park recruits the lifeguards to help create a realistic stunt show.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Neely and Mitch help a manic-depressive bodyboarder; Mitch and Sams relationship is tested; Manny helps Newman meet the woman of his dreams.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Cody realizes he has been taking his relationship with C.J. for granted when a secret admirer comes to her rescue after an accident.

programme.title: An Doimhne
programme.desc: Tha Stoirm Catatumbo a leudachadh gu luath is tha an suidheachadh a fàs air leth cunnartach - ach dè tha ga adhbharachadh? Feumaidh Na Neachdan seo a rannsachadh airson faighinn a-mach dè a th air a chùlaibh! The Catatumbo Storm is expanding rapidly, and the situation is getting very dangerous - but what has caused it? The Neachdan must investigate it to find out what is behind it.

programme.title: Na Speuran
programme.desc: Astronaut Emily shares some interesting facts about the sun. Tha rudan inntinneach aig an Speuradair Emily ri innse dhuinn mun ghrèin.

programme.title: An Saoghal Droil aig Pòl Ploc
programme.sub-title: An t-Uilebheist Eudach Uaine/Green Eyed Monster

programme.title: An Saoghal Droil aig Pòl Ploc

programme.title: Trusadh
programme.sub-title: Mo Mhac-Meanmna Otasamach - My Autistic Imagination
programme.desc: Tha an sgrìobhadair, seinneadair agus neach-ealain, M. Eòghainn MacGriogar, a gabhail turas gus coinneachadh ri luchd-ealain òtasamach coltach rithe fhèin. Ann an còmhradh le iomadh seòrsa neach-ealain, leithid luchd-ciùil, peantairean, dealbhaichean agus dannsairean, cluinnidh i mar a tha ealain is cruthaileachd air saoibhreachadh an cuid beatha fhad s a tha mi-thuigs is dìth ruigsinneachd a gabhail buaidh.

programme.title: Seòid a Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves

programme.title: Spring Walks
programme.desc: The Yorkshire Dales National Park provides the perfect backdrop for comedian and actor Nina Wadia as she explores a rich landscape and expresses deep thoughts. Nina unwinds and reflects in the dramatic spring setting of Swaledale. A keen walker, Nina has travelled the world, but her first trip to the Yorkshire Dales has a profound effect. Nina also tests her skills in a new language when she meets a village elder who speaks Swardle, the historic local dialect.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: A professor develops an extraordinary relationship with an octopus when he invites it to live in his home. The octopus, called Heidi, unravels puzzles, recognises individual humans and even watches TV with the family.

programme.title: Chauvet: Humanitys First Great Masterpiece
programme.desc: Documentary that follows a 30-year archaeological, geological and artistic adventure as scientists endeavour to understand and conserve the extraordinary art of the Chauvet cave.

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: Egg takes his work worries to a therapist, but finds that football is the only way forward. Meanwhile, Miles thinks that his days are numbered when Delilah agrees to take an HIV test, and Anna gets an interesting offer when she uses her feminine charms to get work.

programme.title: Animal Park

programme.title: Farm 999

programme.desc: A criminal gang targets northern farms, a huge barn fire threatens cattle in Leicestershire, and Paul Colgan reunites a farmer with the air ambulance crew who saved her life.

programme.title: Crimewatch Caught
programme.desc: When police arrive at a fatal collision, they must establish if its an accident or deadly driving. And officers in Durham deal with the tragic aftermath of a drunk driver.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.sub-title: Celebrity Special with Martel and Nitro from Gladiators
programme.desc: Martel and Nitro are duelling with each other over ideas at a studio flat in London. Martin is also in Somerset, where another character property is ripe for reinvention.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: In Surrey, Alison wants to downsize a lifetime of clutter that has filled the family home and garage. She hopes that by selling heirlooms and treasures she will be able to take her two grown-up children on a holiday of a lifetime.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: House-hunting in the gorgeous Devon countryside, Jules Hudson helps two retired drama teachers trade busy Sheffield for some coastal calm. Jules has some splendid properties up his sleeve, including a lovely period cottage boasting a magnificent garden just a stones throw from the beach. Along the way, he also explores a 550-acre rewilding project brimming with wildflower meadows and teeming with wildlife.

programme.title: The Repair Shop: On the Road
programme.sub-title: Devon - Emergency Surgery for Daisy the Cow
programme.desc: The team face their quirkiest fix yet - a giant fibreglass cow lantern. Also, luthier Julyan brings a banjulele back to life, and Dom gets a crash course in brilliant cutting.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: House-hunting in the gorgeous Devon countryside, Jules Hudson helps two retired drama teachers trade busy Sheffield for some coastal calm. Jules has some splendid properties up his sleeve, including a lovely period cottage boasting a magnificent garden just a stones throw from the beach. Along the way, he also explores a 550-acre rewilding project brimming with wildflower meadows and teeming with wildlife.

programme.title: Situationships with Sophie and Christine

programme.title: Martin Compstons Norwegian Fling
programme.desc: Journeying through Norways fjord landscapes, Martin and Phil go back in time at Viking Valley, where they discover that the fearsome Norse warriors enjoyed a surprisingly relaxed home life when they returned from their pillaging and fighting.

programme.title: Scot Squad
programme.desc: Criminally funny capers featuring Scotlands funniest force on their most hilarious manoeuvres. Featuring Chief Commissioner Cameron Meikelsons finest pearls of blue-sky thinking, and some memorable public interaction down at the cop shop.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: The team are at the Museum of Natural History in Oxford. Steve takes a look inside some of the top predators featured on `Deadly 60 to find out what makes them so deadly. The skeletons and jaws of crocodiles, snakes and some of the most lethal sharks on the planet hold the key as to their deadly prowess.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall looks at animals that are both deadly and endangered. Threatened with habitat loss, Steve explores why mighty animals like the chimpanzee, the mountain gorilla and the harpy eagle are so few in number, while in Madagascar he meets the most bizarre animal hes ever seen, the Aye Aye.

programme.title: Waterloo Road
programme.desc: It is the final day of reckoning for Waterloo Road as they await the decision of the LEA. Will the school be closed?

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: Its week 7, and the candidates take on a classic task - TV selling. The teams select products which they hope will win over the nation. They then take to the airwaves, hoping to sell their socks off on a TV shopping channel - all while Lord Sugar watches on. Theres a dramatic boardroom showdown, and one candidate risks it all to keep their place in the process.

programme.title: The Trials of Pa Salieu
programme.desc: After prison, Coventry musician Pa Salieu returns with renewed purpose and takes the stage at Glastonbury. While he fights to rebuild his career, can music save him again?

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: Tensions run high ahead of final exams. Omar has a big secret, Femi faces suspicion, and Toby fears for his future. As the hockey girls face their enemies, Leahs plans blow up.

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: Its a big end for the boarders as they race to save Leah from making a huge mistake. Femi is given an ultimatum by Cheddars dad, Omar gets brave, and Toby has a tough decision.

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy receives some terrible news that reminds him to live life to its fullest. Will he manage the biggest physical challenge yet?

programme.desc: Zoe Ball is in Kettering, showing busy beautician Lucie some clever digital detox tricks that could improve family life, reduce stress and even boost her brain power.

programme.title: The Repair Shop: On the Road
programme.desc: Mark battles rust and dead wiring to preserve Prestwicks derelict art deco picture palace, and Dom picks up an heirloom accordion from Shetlander Victoria.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Its sun, sea and sand in this episode as Alistair Appleton helps a couple to trade in city life for a coastal home in Cornwall. With golden beaches and a nearby golf club high on their wish list, will they land a hole in one or get stuck in a sandy bunker? Along the way, Alistair encounters a mischievous ice-cream-snatching seagull and visits a regenerative farm focused on nourishing land, body and mind.

programme.title: Gladiators

programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Thirty players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight contestants compete in a series of fast-paced quiz battles in an attempt to eliminate their opponents from the days game and walk away with a cash prize.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio.

programme.title: Clive Myries Italian Roadtrip
programme.desc: Clive swerves Tuscanys famous posh villas to head to the mediaeval town of Barga, where over half the locals have Scottish connections. Staying in a mansion thats now a Glaswegian-run BandB, Clive finds out why Italians love a fish supper, finds himself cooking dinner for 14, and joins an expedition to discover the forgotten quartz quarry that helped make Tuscanys capital Florence so beautiful.

programme.title: Monkey Planet
programme.desc: George investigates the close-knit family groups of primates, has a very close encounter with some hunting chimpanzees, and sees how barbary macaques steal babies to impress their boss.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Among the turrets and terraces of Eastnor Castle in Herefordshire, Michael Aspel and the team find some bizarre objects, including a huge pocket knife, and a WWI pack with a bullet lodged in it.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In Blackburn, Anita Manning values a gold charm bracelet, and Philip Serrell takes a shine to an unusual globe. Paul Martin finds about the remarkable life of classical singer Kathleen Ferrier.

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Each week a group of four celebrities compete to test their knowledge in a series of quick-paced rounds of games. Host, Richard Osman then cross-examines the celebrities as they strive to increase their scores and match Richards mental prowess.

programme.desc: Four chefs compete for Northern Ireland with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by the British film industry. Who will be eliminated?

As preparations for the new show progress, will Rahul decide on a happy ending?

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The gang heads to Barbados for a palaeontology convention where Ross is the keynote speaker. Rachel harbours steamy thoughts about Joey while David prepares to propose to Phoebe.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One After Joey and Rachel Kiss
programme.desc: While the friends are still in Barbados, Joey tries to tell Ross about his feelings for Rachel and Ross wants to confess to kissing Joeys girlfriend Charlie, but it is easier said than done. Meanwhile, Monica, Chandler and Phoebe can hear their amorous conversations through the hotels paper-thin walls. Also, Monicas hair problems go from bad to worse, and Phoebe has some bad news for Mikes girlfriend on her birthday.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Is Fine
programme.desc: Ross drinks too much wine while dining with Rachel, Joey and Charlie. Elsewhere, Frank Jr cannot cope with his precocious triplets and pays Phoebe a visit.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel and Joey nervously anticipate their first real date, Monica and Phoebe try to avoid an obnoxious friend with a fake British accent, and Ross emerges from a tanning booth with a two-toned appearance.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel gets Monica and Chandler to delay their long-awaited trip to Vermont for Emmas first birthday bash, but problems ensue when Emma falls asleep and a racy cake turns up.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachels Sister Baby-Sits
programme.desc: Rachel regrets trusting her self-indulgent little sister Amy with Emma, Monica regrets asking Joey to write a recommendation letter to the child adoption agency for her, and Phoebe accidentally thwarts the anniversary surprise Mike has in store for her.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross applies for a palaeontology grant, but discovers that Dr Hobart - who allocates such funding - used to go out with his girlfriend Charlie. Meanwhile, Mike wants Phoebe to get rid of one of her bizarre paintings, but neither Rachel nor Monica is keen to take it, and Chandler lies to Joey about watching his audition tape.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Home Study
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler fuss over their apartment as they expect a visit from the adoption worker. Meanwhile, Phoebe and Mike opt for a small wedding and donate the extra money to a childrens charity.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Late Thanksgiving
programme.desc: Monica reluctantly agrees to cook Thanksgiving dinner, but locks everyone out when they turn up late as Joey and Ross went to a hockey game while Rachel and Phoebe took Emma to a baby beauty contest. Monica also receives some adoption news.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Birth Mother
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler are forced to pretend to be a reverend and a doctor when their prospective childs birth mother gets the wrong end of the stick. Meanwhile, Joeys date with Phoebes friend doesnt go well, and Ross is left with an inappropriate outfit for a date with a sexy salesgirl.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Gets Caught
programme.desc: Phoebe and Rachel see Chandler getting into a car with a mystery woman and follow them to a large house in the suburbs. They tell Monica of their fears and are shocked by the truth.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where the Stripper Cries
programme.desc: Joey appears as a celebrity contestant on `The $100,000 Pyramid with its actual host Donny Osmond, but Joeys dubious IQ costs his partner the prize money. Meanwhile, Monica and Rachel hire an ungainly stripper, Roy - without seeing him first - for Phoebes bachelorette party. Also, Chandler and Ross attend a college reunion and meet an attractive woman they both promised not to date during school.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Its Phoebe and Mikes big day, but all does not go to plan. Wedding planner Monica is driving the bride to distraction, New York is hit by its worst blizzard for 20 years, and Joey is taking his role standing in for Phoebes incarcerated father far too seriously. Chandler and Ross are upset that neither of them has a role, until Mike needs to replace one of his groomsmen.

programme.title: Father Stu
programme.desc: After surviving a motorcycle accident, a former boxer and longtime agnostic starts to wonder if he can use his second chance to help others, leading to the surprising realisation that hes meant to be a Catholic priest.

programme.title: Rons Gone Wrong
programme.desc: In a world where walking, talking, digitally connected bots have become childrens best friends, 11-year-old Ron finds that his robot buddy doesnt quite work.

programme.title: Anastasia
programme.desc: Based on the story of Anastasia, grand-daughter of the last Tsar of Russia. When the family is killed, only Anastasia and her grandmother escape, but they are separated. Years later, conmen Dimitri and Vladimir hear of a reward for the return of Anastasia to her grandmother and begin preparing Anya to play the part, little realising that the teenage girl is, in fact, the real Anastasia.

programme.title: Sword of Sherwood Forest
programme.desc: Lively reworking of the exploits of Robin Hood who foils the villainous Sheriff of Nottingham and the Earl of Newarks dastardly plan to murder the Archbishop of Canterbury.

programme.title: Men in Black International Interview Special
programme.desc: Stars Chris Hemsworth and Tessa Thompson, director F Gary Gray and producers Walter Parkes and Laurie Macdonald talk about bringing the series back for a fourth part.

programme.title: The Golden Voyage of Sinbad
programme.desc: Sinbads adventure pits him against a master magician whos trying to reach the Fountain of Destiny on the lost land of Lemuria in a bid to be crowned the new leader of Marabia. Sinbad and his crew must battle an array of monsters before finally coming face to face with his foe.

programme.title: Men in Black International Interview Special
programme.desc: Stars Chris Hemsworth and Tessa Thompson, director F Gary Gray and producers Walter Parkes and Laurie Macdonald talk about bringing the series back for a fourth part.

programme.title: Cheaper by the Dozen
programme.desc: Tom Baker decides to move his family to the big city so he can take his dream job of coaching the football team of his smalltown college. However, he discovers that its not going to be easy when his wife Kate departs on a book tour, leaving Tom alone with the 12 children.

programme.title: Fishermans Friends: One and All
programme.desc: After the highs of performing on the pyramid stage at Glastonbury, the group struggle with their second album. During a divisive tour of South Australia, they will trace their ancestors and embrace a new community, and discover their musical DNA.

programme.title: Nuns on the Run
programme.desc: Small-time crooks are forced to hide out in a convent disguised as nuns, when they are pursued by gangsters and the police after parting their ruthless boss Robert Patterson from the £1 million he stole from a Triad gang in London.

programme.title: Paranormal Activity: Next of Kin
programme.desc: Margot, a young woman who was abandoned by her mother as a baby, travels to a secluded Amish community with a documentary film crew seeking answers about her mother and extended family.

programme.title: Voyage to the Bottom of the Sea
programme.desc: A belt of radiation goes out of control and threatens to melt the Earth, leaving Admiral Nelson and his prototype nuclear submarine to salvage the situation.

programme.title: Men In Black 3 Interview Special
programme.desc: Will Smith, Josh Brolin and director Barry Sonnenfeld talk about the third of their sci-fi comedy adventures.

programme.title: Turner and Hooch
programme.desc: Turner is a meticulously groomed, excruciatingly well-organised detective working in a small California coastal town. When local character John McIntyre is murdered by drug smugglers, the only witness is McIntyres slobbery, smelly mutt Hooch. Turner, who despises dogs in general and Hooch in particular, is compelled to put the cantankerous dog up as his house guest.

programme.title: Oliver!
programme.desc: Endearing film version of Lionel Barts successful stage musical based on Dickenss tale of the fluctuating fortunes of an orphan boy. Ron Moody stands out as the wily old scrounger Fagin in a series of well-crafted set-pieces and atmospheric musical numbers, and a tribe of child stars brings the 19th-century London underworld vividly to life.

programme.title: Men in Black International Interview Special
programme.desc: Stars Chris Hemsworth and Tessa Thompson, director F Gary Gray and producers Walter Parkes and Laurie Macdonald talk about bringing the series back for a fourth part.

programme.title: Hidden Figures
programme.desc: NASA mathematicians Katherine Johnson, Dorothy Vaughan and Mary Jackson cross gender and race lines to help launch astronaut John Glenn into outer space.

After crashing on an unknown planet, two survivors quickly discover theyre actually stranded on Earth, 65 million years ago. With only one chance at a rescue, they must make their way across an unknown terrain riddled with prehistoric creatures.

programme.title: The Predator
programme.desc: From the outer reaches of space to the small-town streets of suburbia, the hunt comes home. The universes most lethal hunters are stronger, smarter and deadlier than ever before, having genetically upgraded themselves with DNA from other species. When a boy accidentally triggers their return to Earth, only a ragtag crew of ex-soldiers and an evolutionary biologist can prevent the end of the human race.

programme.title: Seven Psychopaths
programme.desc: A struggling screenwriter finds himself inadvertently entangled in the criminal underworld when his knucklehead friends abduct a mobsters beloved Shih Tzu.

programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes a hearty fish pie, helped by his son Buddy, with a delicate fish curry made with coconut milk and spinach for the sauce.

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: In the final programme of the series, Jamie Oliver cooks his versions of three comfort food classics. Theres ham, egg and chips, with a Jamaican jerk rub for the ham and served with caramelized pineapple. His ultimate chicken kebab is served with hummus and mango amba sauce.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Today, Inas sharing all the secrets for effortless entertaining, with recipes for chilli tortilla chips, slow roasted fillet of beef with basil and parmesan mayonnaise and truffled mashed potatoes.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its bread week in the tent and the bakers must create quick breads for the signature challenge. The technical challenge is baguettes and for the showstopper, the bakers must make edible 3D Bread sculptures.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Theres nine bakers left in the tent and the signature challenge is a French classic - creme brulee. The technical challenge tasks the bakers with making a Spanische windtorte and the showstopper calls for a stack of tiered cheesecakes.

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure
programme.desc: The time has come to cross the border from Scotland to England and where better to begin this leg of the journey than James home country of Yorkshire. Here hes joined by Michelin starred chef Lisa Allen. His first stop is Whitby where hes popping into his favourite fish and chip shop before cooking tikka mackerel buns with fennel and apple salad for the crowds at Whitby bandstand. Lisa is paying a visit to The Black Swan.

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James continues his British adventure in Lancashire with top local chef Lisa Allen in the passenger seat of his trusty red mini. Their first stop is an incredible food hall in Lisas home town of Clitheroe. Here they shop and then cook together a fantastic BBQd john dory with kohlrabi and fennel salad. Next, Lisa is in the driving seat as she takes the mini to a local buffalo farm where theyre producing some interesting cheese from buffalo milk.

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. Paul cant afford to make any mistakes and is pushing himself with a technically challenging baked alaska.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North West chefs must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. The guest judge is Greg Rusedski, former British number one, who understands the high standards expected at Wimbledon.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning banquet champion Tommy Banks takes on two ambitious newcomers Danny Parker and Josh Overington. They start by finding out whois the surprise veteran judge and who will be marking their dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The atmosphere in the kitchen gets even more heated, the three chefs are cooking their fish courses. Josh Overington, creates a decadent dish inspired by the prestige of the Wimbledon Championships.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are cooking their main courses and are all taking risks to try and win a place at the banquet. Danny Parker, is trying to recreate the flavours of an American barbecue but the veteran chef is dubious about one of his ingredients.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. All the chefs are using unusual ingredients in their desserts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs from the North East must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort, Andi Oliver, and guest Judy Murray.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the Scottish regional heats and returning chefs to the competition Michael Bremner and Ally McGrath battle ambitious newcomer Angela Malik for a place in the national finals. This time they are cooking their starters.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The atmosphere in the kitchen gets even more heated and the veteran chef is expecting perfection from the fish course. Ally McGrath creates an inventive dish based on his summer memories of rock pooling in the River Tweed.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Angela Malik is in her element, cooking a tiffin box based on memories of summer picnics. Michael Bremner is taking a huge risk, and Ally is creating an inventive dish based on a summer barbeque.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. Angela Malik is cooking a technically challenging dessert.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two Scottish chefs must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver. They are joined by guest judge Gary Parsons.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Determined newcomers Paul Croasdale and Nick Brodie take on returning chef Phil Carmichael for a place in the national finals. Its Phils third time in the competition and this year hes determined to make it to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The pressure in the kitchen is high as the chefs prepare their fish courses. Paul Croasdale is trying to recreate the elegance of Wimbledon with luxury ingredients such as king crab and scallops.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the main course and all three chefs are cooking Welsh lamb in hope of impressing. Phil is taking a risk with barbecued lamb and in an attempt to elevate a humble barbecue to banquet level, hes serving it with many accompaniments.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course and the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges so all three chefs are feeling the pressure. Phil Carmichael creates a tennis ball dessert that combines two British classics, Eton mess and trifle.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two winning chefs from the Wales region must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges and guest judge Dan Bloxham, Wimbledons Master of Ceremonies.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With two spots left in the National Finals, its the turn of the Central chefs to battle it out. Pip Lacey, head chef at the Michelin starred Murano in London, tried her luck in the competition two years ago. Shes takes on two newcomers.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With the chefs hoping to impress, they each take risks. Pip Lacey combines lobster with strawberry in a tribute to strawberries and cream. Ryan Simpsons also using lobster in his recreation of a BBQ and has invented a technique to create his chips.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Today the chefs cook their main courses and have very different takes on the Taste of Summer brief. Ryan Simpson hopes to elevate a ploughmans lunch into a dish worthy of the banquet, and Nick Deverell-Smith celebrates a meat he thinks is underused.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges. Pip Laceys pineapple dessert uses coriander. Nick Deverell-Smith also pays tribute to the pineapple, creating a dessert featuring a rice pudding made with cous-cous.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs whove made it through cook their four courses again. They must impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort, Andi Oliver and guest judge Jordanne Whiley, one of Britains most successful tennis players.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs meet their surprise veteran judge and cook their starters. Eddie is hoping to do better than last year, Joery is hoping to impress with a tribute to Richard Krajicek, and Tommy is creating a warm salad.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Joery Castel is looking to triumph by combing mackerel and cucumber. Tommy Heaney hopes his Murray Mound is a fitting tribute to Andi Murray, Eddie Attwell is creating five different strawberry elements to accompany the scallops in his dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tommy Heaneys BBQ dish is a surf and turf combination, Joery Castels aiming to recreate a more American style BBQ and Eddie Attwells taking a risk with an unusual meat.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tommy Heaneys BBQ dish is a surf and turf combination, Joery Castels aiming to recreate a more American style BBQ and Eddie Attwells taking a risk with an unusual meat.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs whove made it through cook their four courses again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver who are joined this week by tennis greatest entertainer, Mansour Bahrami.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With Finals Week ahead of them, judge Andi Oliver first summons the chefs to Wimbledon to take a look at where they could be cooking if they win. The judges are joined by guest judge Sue Barker, host of the Wimbledon coverage for more than 20 years.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs must cook their fish courses but with some exceptionally high scoring dishes in the regional heats, each chef will need to deliver perfection in order to see off the competition. The judges are joined by guest judge Tim Henman.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is main course day and with only two spots remaining on the banquet menu, the competition is fierce. As the barbecues are fired up, all 8 chefs are feeling the heat. The judges are joined by guest judgeand Paralympic gold medallist, Gordon Reid.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is dessert day and with just one place remaining at the Wimbledon banquet, each chef is giving everything they have got. The judges are joined by guest judge Annabel Croft, former British Number One and Junior Wimbledon Champion.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the grand finale and the winning chefs must now cook their dishes at the banquet. Once in the kitchen, the chefs face the huge challenge of scaling up their dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: London and South East - Starter and Fish Courses
programme.desc: Competing for the top spot are 3 new chefs to the competition representing some of the most exciting culinary talent from London, Paul Walsh, Luke Selby and Ben Marks.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: London and South East Mains and Desserts
programme.desc: The three chefs are halfway through their heat and the pressure is mounting. Only two will proceed to Fridays judging and have a chance to represent the London and the South East region at the regional finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London chefs must cook their four course menus again. The chefs need to impress the formidable judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge.

programme.sub-title: North East Starter and Fish Courses

programme.title: Ancient Egypt: Life and Death in the Valley of the Kings
programme.desc: Egyptologist Dr Joann Fletcher discovers what life was really like for ordinary Egyptians 3,500 years ago, during the height of the incredible civilisation of ancient Egypt. Joann explores how people lived during this time, from the tightly packed houses and villages of the common people, to the clothes they wore and the food they ate.

programme.title: Ancient Egypt: Life and Death in the Valley of the Kings
programme.desc: Egyptologist Dr Joann Fletcher reveals a strange and mysterious world: the ancient Egyptian afterlife. To them waking life was just a dress rehearsal. Joann clambers into rarely visited tombs, explores a treasure trove of long-buried objects and examines spectacular mummies to discover just why the Egyptians spent a fortune preparing for death.

programme.title: Ancient Giants: Legends of Anakims and Nephilim
programme.desc: Anthropologists may have discovered an ancient 7-14 foot tall giant human subgroup.

programme.title: Barbarossa: The Lost Diaries
programme.desc: Lieutenant Sander undertakes an arduous journey to Leningrad, revealing the harsh German east campaign of Operation Barbarossa. He notes down his experiences in a diary that provides us a look at the attack on the Soviet Union.

programme.title: Barbarossa: The Lost Diaries
programme.desc: Lieutenant Sander undertakes an arduous journey to Leningrad, revealing the harsh German east campaign of Operation Barbarossa. He notes down his experiences in a diary that provides us a look at the attack on the Soviet Union.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: In the run-up to September 1939, political and economic chaos in the aftermath of World War I allowed Hitler in Germany, Mussolini in Italy and ultra-nationalists in imperial Japan to seize absolute power.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Germany took just four weeks to conquer Poland, using a new form of warfare - Blitzkrieg. Six armoured tank units known as Panzer divisions, supported by the Luftwaffe, quickly broke through Polish defences.

programme.title: America Unearthed
programme.desc: Remnants of the Berkeley Hills Walls, a massive stone wall that spans a distance of over fifty miles. The wall segments go up to six feet high in some places and are made of tightly-fitted limestone boulders.

programme.title: America Unearthed
programme.desc: In 1874, Lt. Col. George sent Custer on a scouting mission to the Black Hills area. He reportedly discovered gold there, which sparked off a gold rush of settlers flooding the sacred Native American lands.

programme.title: America Unearthed
programme.desc: Scott Wolter investigates a 3000-pound boulder hidden inside the Chicago History Museums secret storage location.

programme.title: Operation Carpetbagger: The Secret Mission to End World War II
programme.desc: Chronicling Operation Carpetbagger, one of the most top-secret missions of World War II.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Explores Hitlers assault on the Soviet Union in the summer of 1941, Operation Barbarossa and his attack on the Caucasus in 1942.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: The attack on Pearl Harbour brought the sleeping giant, America, into the war, and saw the emergence of the aircraft carrier as the pre-eminent weapon of the seas.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Mussolini wanted to recreate the Roman Empire, but proved to be a poor military strategist and his incompetence contributed to Hitlers eventual defeat.

Britains Greatest Obsessions

programme.title: Cracking the Code
programme.desc: Revealing the greatest codes known to humanity and the brilliant minds that manage to solve them.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones explores the turbulent history of Carrickfergus, one of the most ancient castles in Northern Ireland and a stronghold that holds the key to understanding the tempestuous relationship between Britain and Ireland.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Edinburghs handsome hilltop bastion is Scotlands most popular paid tourist attraction. With a slew of sieges to its name, it is no surprise that the site boasts some formidable defences, and Dan puts one heavy duty cannon through its paces.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores the story of Stewart Britain by visiting Stirling, the home of the Scottish royals who eventually took the English throne in 1603 and created Great Britain.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice is in Lincoln, getting to grips with life in medieval England. She visits the magnificent cathedral that was once the tallest building in the world and re-lives the battle at Lincoln Castle.

programme.title: Invasion: The Normans
programme.desc: In 12th-century Ireland, provincial kings vie for supremacy. When two of these power players drive a rival into exile, he seeks help from Henry II, the Anglo-Norman king of England. Henrys vassal Strongbow and other Norman allies then invade Ireland with frightening power.

programme.title: Invasion: The Normans
programme.desc: Following the course of the invasion, as Strongbow wins victory after victory. In 1171, Henry II himself arrives to takes control of Ireland, and grants land there to settlers from Britain. Now, archaeology and cutting-edge science reveal their fascinating lives.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice discovers things at Norwich Castle Museum which highlight facts about Tudor life, Anglo-Saxon childbirth rituals, and the Pompeii of the Bronze Age.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: A look back at the best archaeological findings of 2014, including the Barrow Clump, Ipplepen and Winchester sites.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Edinburghs handsome hilltop bastion is Scotlands most popular paid tourist attraction. With a slew of sieges to its name, it is no surprise that the site boasts some formidable defences, and Dan puts one heavy duty cannon through its paces.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones explores the history of Cardiff castle over more than 2,000 years and reveals how it became transformed from a strategically important Roman and Norman stronghold to a lavish fairy-tale Victorian home.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice tells the story of Norman England by studying the history of Winchester, Britains most Norman city. Alice attempts to storm a castle in chain mail, investigates the origins of modern surgery and tucks in to an appetising eel pie.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In Norwich, the UKs most Tudor city, Professor Alice Roberts learns about religious intolerance, uncovers the Norwich FC canarys origins and experiences humiliating Tudor justice.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: In the late 18th century, there was a sure-fire way to earn a living along the Cornish coast: smuggling. The great walking country is home to tiny secretive harbours, beaches and secluded coves which were once ideal for the infamous illicit imports.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson visits Liverpool and Lancashire for a towpath exploration of grand industrial engineering along the majestic Leeds and Liverpool Canal.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: A look at the biggest collection of Roman writing tablets in Britain, giving insight into what Roman London was really like. Off the coast of Kent, a dive into the English Channel reveals the biggest marine excavation since the Mary Rose, an 18th-century East India Company ship packed with silver.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice discovers the well-preserved writing tablets, swords and domestic items left by Romans at Vindolanda during a time of British rebellion. On the Scottish island of Iona, there are traces of a long-lost monastery and pilgrimage site that was originally built by the legendary Saint Columba, and has been compared to Jerusalem. In the east of Scotland, a weapons hoard belonging to a wealthy Bronze Age warrior is unearthed.

programme.title: Shipwrecks: When History Resurfaces
programme.sub-title: Shipwrecked Sailors Of The Roman Empire
programme.desc: Kevin Sempe dives in search of wrecks harking back to the Roman Empires greatness. The journey begins with the Nave Romana.

programme.title: Shipwrecks: When History Resurfaces
programme.desc: Kevin Sempe explores the bay of Saint-Pierre in Martinique, where ships smouldered and sank following the devastating volcanic eruption of 1902.

programme.title: Celts: The Untold Story

programme.title: Celts: The Untold Story

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A bungalow in Callington in Cornwall, a three-bed semi in Willenhall near Wolverhampton and a terraced house in Hertfordshires Watford are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Dion and Martel meet the new owners, and hear their plans for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander talks to experienced developers who deliver a stunning renovation on a subsidence-troubled semi in Coulsdon in Surrey, Dion Dublin meets two brothers with ambitious plans for a commercial property in Derby, and Martel Maxwell meets with two golfing friends turned developers who plan to turn round a one-bedroom flat in Telford in Shropshire in record time.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two-bed detached house ripe for extending in Bilston near Wolverhampton, a cosy cottage in Nettlestead Green in Kent and a terraced house in Etruria in Stoke-on-Trent without a bathroom or an inside loo are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A garage with development potential in Margate in Kent, a ground-floor flat in a Victorian house in Shepherds Bush, London, and a piece of land ripe for building on in Birmingham are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The trio look at a family home in Kent, a former bar in Llanelli in Wales and a terrace in Croydon, all sold at auction. Will any of the developers make a return on their investments?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A three-bedroom semi in Crewe, a top-floor flat in London and a terraced house in Oldham are sold under the hammer. All the owners are happy with the prices they paid, but will any of them be able to turn a profit at the end of the projects?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: While developers in Croydon in Surrey and Donnington in Shropshire wrestle with the question of whether to lose a bedroom each for a better placed kitchen or bathroom, two friends in Derby consider creating two more in their six-bedroom house.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two brothers buy a rundown cottage in Probus in Cornwall, a father buys a flat in Mitcham in Surrey as a long-term investment for his young daughter and a seasoned property developer buys a house in Tunstall in Stoke-on-Trent for her daughter.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion is in Plaistow in London looking around a three-bedroom semi, Lucy is checking out a small piece of land in Crayford in Kent, and Martin meets a father and son who have bought a small house with a big garden in Wheelock in Cheshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A former miners cottage in Aberaman in South Wales and semis in Adderley Green in Stoke and Streatham in South London are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion look around auction buys in Birmingham, Oldham and Gravesend and discover what the new owners plan to do with their investments.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion travel the country meeting developers hoping to make some money from their auction buys. Today they are in Stoke, Croydon and Warrington hearing tales from the auction rooms.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A former doctors surgery in need of TLC is converted into a family home in Houghton-le-spring and a flat in a 1930s block of flats in Edgware and a detached house in a woodland garden in Ashford in Kent are also given makeovers.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Theres a family home in Consett in County Durham, a rental investment in Derby and an auction buy in Beckenham in London which will be used to run a new business.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion looks round a large Victorian terrace in Plumstead, Martin is in Mickleover in Derby hearing a father and daughters plans for a flat and Lucy discovers what happened to a plot of land she saw five and a half years ago.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin looks round a bungalow in a stunning setting in the Lake District while Dion has a look at a flat close to Nottingham city centre. Lucy meets a woman who mistakenly thought she was buying a piece of land in Folkestone in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy looks at a flat in Kilburn in London with one of the worst cases of damp she has ever seen, while Martin and Dion are in the Midlands looking round semis with potential.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat with huge potential in Londons Earlsfield, and terraced houses in Wombourne near Wolverhampton and Ynyswen near Treorchy in Wales are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin is impressed with the potential of two shops and three flats in Tonypandy in Wales while Dion looks round a two-bedroom maisonette in Dawley and Lucy meets a first time developer with ambitious plans for an end terrace in Londons Forest Gate.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A first-floor one-bedroom flat in Londons Stepney Green, a three-bed semi in Dartford in Kent and a one-bedroom flat on the 17th floor of a block in Salford are all sold under the hammer.

programme.desc: Maria struggles to tell Liam the news as she tries to come to terms with whats happened. Roy is flummoxed by Beckys change of heart over Jason, and a nervous Audrey meets Ted.

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Faced with the death of a police informer on an estate riven with racial tension, Frost finds his patience pushed to the limit.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: There are problems for the police when PC Bellamy has to organise a talent contest, while Nick and Kates relationship is put to the test when one of her old flames, Peter, arrives in town. To make matters worse, Nick is called out to investigate a hit-and-run accident, and his inquiries point to Peter as a prime suspect.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick investigates the case of a baby stolen from its pram outside a shop. The prime suspects are a group of hippies who were close nearby, however, the childs grandmother is also under suspicion. Meanwhile, Kate is treating a woman who is driven to despair by her inability to conceive.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a solicitor falls from a roof to his death, Barnaby and Scott investigate whether it was suicide or murder. They discover that a rival lawyer was spreading harmful rumours about the dead man, but when this man is drugged and killed as well, the motive looks like revenge - but for what?

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britains best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britains best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liam leaves for work as a distraught Maria packs her bag for the hospital, unable to find a way of telling him about the baby. Meanwhile, Jack learns Paul has been charged.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maria continues to keep Liam in the dark as she heads back to the Street. Bill lets slip to a perplexed Gail about Audrey meeting Ted, and Liz refuses to go on Vernons cruise.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Lord Ashfordlys winter pheasant shoot is disrupted when the entire contents of his game larder are stolen. Nick investigates an apparent crime wave in Aidensfield after a mysterious spate of burglaries, his enquiries make him believe that an incestuous relationship is the reason behind the thefts.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The annual funfair comes to Aidensfield, causing much excitement but some ill feeling too. When the proprietor of a nearby caravan site complains about the noise and mess, Nick tells her there is nothing he can do. Then a five-year-old boy goes missing.

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A contractor is found dead on the steps of Collingwood Memorial after being attacked. Vera then discovers that the contractor recently testified as a key witness in a violent assault case, and wonders if the two things are connected.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones are sampling the local cider at Midsomer Abbas spring fair when the body of a missing tax inspector is found floating in the cider vat. Meanwhile, publican Samuel Quested says that he wants to restore an ancient rite called the Stag in which men go in search of women from another village in order to refresh the gene pool. As Barnaby and Jones investigate the murder they uncover an array of motives.

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: When a local businesswoman is brutally bludgeoned to death, it appears to be a blackmail plot gone wrong. However, as the bodies begin to pile up, Lewis and Hathaway realise that there is a decades-old secret at the heart of the case - and they must uncover it if they are to stop the murders.

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: There is a vacancy for Inspector, and the job has been offered to Jack.

programme.title: International Friendly Football LIVE
programme.desc: Croatia take on Brazil as part of the Road to 26 series of international friendlies leading into the 2026 FIFA World Cup from the USA. Brazil have won the World Cup more than any other nation, although their last success was in 2002. Croatia have recent good form in the tournament as they reached the semi-finals in 2022, beating Brazil in the quarter-finals to get there, and also reached the final in 2018, losing to France. The match takes place at the Camping World Stadium in Orlando, Florida.

programme.desc: The second visit is to The East Walls Hotel in Chichester, West Sussex, owned by history fans Celia and Damon Haughey who have rejected the boutique label and branded themselves `bespoke.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit is to The Wheatsheaf in Sandbach, Cheshire, where young and ambitious owner Jake Pare, and joint general manager Chris Baldwin want to prove that boutique is best.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to The Shawries Hotel in Blackpool, run by Allison and Dave Shaw. The decor takes Jake and Chris back to their childhood, but Celia and Damon arent fans of the 80s look.

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Car S.O.S

programme.title: Brian Hicks: Weird Mouth Things
programme.desc: The only thing Brian Hicks believes in any more is shortening the lines at Kohls; apart from that, Valentines Day cards can remain unread, dentists can remain mediocre, and the meaning of subdivision names can remain a mystery.

programme.title: Dennis Regan: Dennisland
programme.desc: Dennis Regan performs his signature brand of observational comedy.

programme.title: Drew Allen: Out of Ideas
programme.desc: Cursed with the ability to over-analyze even the most basic of human interactions, comedian Drew Allen has been delighting audiences with his distinct brand of anxious comedy since 2010.

programme.title: Ray Bradshaw: Deaf Comedy Fam
programme.desc: Ray Bradshaw tells a loveable tale of growing up with deaf parents, laced with pranks and playful, witty anecdotes of family life in his signature cheeky Glaswegian humour.

programme.title: Njambi McGrath: Breaking Black
programme.desc: Njambi McGrath offers acerbic anecdotes about life in Kenya, living in a white neighbourhood, why immigrants are attracted to the UK, and how they thrive amongst certain hostilities.

programme.title: Pete Heat: Blimey
programme.desc: Lanky urban wizard Pete Heat uses comedy and mind-blowing tricks to explore that most divisive of subjects: magic itself. Whether it is a visceral experience that loosens ones grasp on reality or a dated, cliche-riddled embarrassment, is TBD.

programme.title: Ali Brice: I tried to be funny but you werent looking
programme.desc: Ali Brice embraces his life after almost losing it. Three years on, hes admitted defeat and is back on his feet and trying again. Through therapy, friends and the magic of sausages.

programme.title: Sian Davies: About Time
programme.desc: Growing up to find ones place in the world: A metamorphic year of adventure, travel and trouble.

programme.title: Archie Maddocks: Matchstick
programme.desc: Cool, smart and humble to boot, Archie Maddocks presents a provocative, unflinching hour of life, love, loss and laughs with his iconic style of candid authenticity.

programme.title: Aaron Simmonds: Disabled Coconut
programme.desc: Wheelchair enthusiast Aaron Simmonds discusses being trolled online for not being disabled enough.

programme.title: Tarot: Cautionary Tales
programme.desc: Come learn life lessons from five people still doing sketch comedy in their 30s, by which they mean its a drain on their bank accounts and they dont talk to their parents about it.

programme.title: Gerry Kyei: NextUp Special
programme.desc: Anecdotal, observational and refreshingly honest in style, Gerrys comedy is enriched by a somewhat unique perspective.

programme.desc: Anecdotal, observational and refreshingly honest in style, Gerrys comedy is enriched by a somewhat unique perspective.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring three treasured family heirlooms, and the memories they hold, back to life. Fine art conservator Lucia Scalisi finds herself in the middle of an unusual mystery, when she sets about restoring a painting by celebrated artist CJ Frost. Brought into the Repair Shop by Andrew Winter, the portrait depicts his mother as a young woman, and having been left languishing in an attic for decades, it has become torn and discoloured over time.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: In Yorkshire, Drew and Al pick through a stash of antiques belonging to a highly regarded dealer. Then, they visit a fourth-generation metalsmith.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: With a swirl of tartan and a burst of the bagpipes, our Scottish tour continues in Fife with antiques experts Natasha Raskin Sharp and Steven Moore. The 1960s Rolls Royce glides the pair through countryside and town as they head from the east coast to central Scotland. Natashas money reserves are dwindling, so she cautiously settles on a bargain piece of Scottish studio pottery. However, a canny buy of an arts and crafts-style lantern pays dividends at the auction in Hamilton.

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: Auctioneer Angus and his team are called out to house clearances, looking through a lifetimes worth of personal possessions, and deciding what to sell at auction, what to recycle and what should be taken to the tip.

However, he goes too far when he kidnaps the queen and imprisons her in an appalling dungeon. The special skills of the glittering and impetuous swordsman Lancelot must be called upon to effect her rescue.

programme.title: Fast and Furious

Heading to LA to track down the culprit, he crosses paths with Brian OConnor, the FBI agent whose undercover infiltration of Torettos gang led to the motorised miscreants exile across the border. The two men temporarily put their differences aside as they investigate the killing.

programme.title: Rampage
programme.desc: Primatologist Davis Okoye shares an unshakable bond with George, an extraordinarily intelligent silverback gorilla thats been in his care since birth. When a genetic experiment goes wrong, it causes George, a wolf and a reptile to grow to a monstrous size. As the mutated beasts embark on a path of destruction, Okoye teams up with a discredited genetic engineer and the military to secure an antidote and prevent a global catastrophe.

programme.title: Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga

While investigating one of Drakes experiments, Eddies body merges with the alien Venom -- leaving him with superhuman strength and power. Twisted, dark and fueled by rage, Venom tries to control the new and dangerous abilities that Eddie finds so intoxicating.

programme.title: Venom: Let There Be Carnage

programme.title: Amadeus

Despite Salieris Machiavellian attempts to frustrate Mozarts progress, the great young composer attracts the favour of the Emperor.

Juni must save his sister, as they battle it out against lifelike 3-D monsters, completing each progressively harder level and ultimately trying to save the youth of the world.

programme.title: Harold and the Purple Crayon
programme.desc: Inside his book, Harold can make anything come to life simply by drawing it. After he grows up and draws himself off the books pages and into the real world, he learns his trusty crayon can set off more hilarious hijinks than he thought possible.

programme.desc: Two siblings set out on a mission to rescue their ex-spy parents. Summoned back to work from their uneventful lives, the adults run into trouble while on duty. Finding himself lost and alone at Paddington Station, he begins to realise that city life is not all he had imagined - until he meets the kindly Brown family, who offer him a temporary haven.

programme.title: Paddington 2
programme.desc: Settled in with the Brown family, Paddington the bear is a popular member of the community who spreads joy and marmalade wherever he goes. One fine day, he spots a pop-up book in an antique shop: the perfect present for his beloved aunts 100th birthday. When a thief steals the prized book, Paddington embarks on an epic quest to unmask the culprit before Aunt Lucys big celebration.

programme.title: Paddington in Peru
programme.desc: Paddington and his adopted family, the Browns, decide to visit his aunt Lucy in Peru, but circumstances lead them towards misadventures in the Amazon rainforest and mountains of Peru.

programme.title: A Minecraft Movie
programme.desc: A mysterious portal pulls four misfits into the Overworld, a bizarre, cubic wonderland that thrives on imagination. To get back home, theyll have to master the terrain while embarking on a magical quest with an unexpected crafter named Steve.

programme.title: Harold and the Purple Crayon
programme.desc: Inside his book, Harold can make anything come to life simply by drawing it. After he grows up and draws himself off the books pages and into the real world, he learns his trusty crayon can set off more hilarious hijinks than he thought possible.

programme.title: The First Wives Club
programme.desc: When Brenda Cushman, Elise Elliot and Annie Paradis are reunited at the funeral of a college friend, they discover they have another thing in common: theyve all been dumped by their husbands, Morty, Bill and Aaron, for a younger model. Vowing revenge, they decide to hit them where it hurts most: the pocket. Using help and their various skills, they begin to pick them off one by on.

programme.title: The Cinema List: Unmissable Sky Originals
programme.desc: Dive into a world of unmissable Sky Original films, from psychological thriller To Catch a Killer to the Oscar-nominated The Unites States vs Billie Holiday.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: An alcoholic millionaire is threatened with losing his inheritance if he does not marry the girl of his parents choosing. Despite his dislike for his prospective bride, he bows to the inevitable only to meet a light-fingered waitress who steals his heart. John Gielgud won an Oscar for his role as Arthurs loyal but outspoken valet.

programme.title: The Blues Brothers
programme.desc: The story of a pair of blues musicians who try to raise the money to save an orphanage by reuniting their old band - nearly writing off Chicago in the process.

programme.title: The Naked Gun: From the Files of Police Squad!
programme.desc: Hard-boiled incompetent cop Frank Drebin must stop the evil Vincent Ludwig from completing his diabolical plan of killing Elizabeth II.

programme.title: The Naked Gun 2 1/2: The Smell of Fear
programme.desc: Lt Frank Debrin uncovers a plot by the polluting energy giants to de-rail ecological plans that could render their products redundant. Theyre led by the evil Quentin Hapsburg who, to make matters worse, is romancing Franks ex, Jane Spencer.

programme.title: The Naked Gun 33 1/3: The Final Insult
programme.desc: Clueless cop Frank Drebin is hauled out of retirement to save the Oscars ceremony from mad bombers. Anna Nicole Smith provides support in this comic sequel.

programme.title: The Big Chill
programme.desc: Reunited at a funeral, several college friends look back at how their lives have changed since the turbulent 1960s.

programme.title: The Cable Guy
programme.desc: After being kicked out of his apartment by his girlfriend, Steven hears that if he slips a cable guy $50, he can get hooked up with free cable - but at what further cost? Can Steven gently reject the cable guys insane attempts to be best buds and also get his girlfriend back?

programme.title: Bad Boys
programme.desc: Two wisecracking Miami cops are forced to swap identities in order to track down $100 million of heroin stolen from the police. Their only lead is a young woman who can identify the culprits, but when they track her down, she mistakes one of the cops for his partner, forcing the pair into a bizarre and chaotic life swap.

programme.title: Batman
programme.desc: In an attempt to avenge the violent death of his parents, Bruce Wayne fights to rid the city of crime and in particular, his nemesis the Joker. However, his defences come down when an attractive journalist tries to track down the truth behind sightings of a huge bat-like figure in the sky.

programme.title: Batman Returns
programme.desc: After Selina Kyle sees more than she should in her bosss office, she has an `accident, falling from the top floor of the building to the street below. Awoken by the licking of cats, she is surprised to find herself still very much alive. She vows to take revenge on her boss and becomes a female avenger, defending women under attack and providing assistance to Batmans efforts to keep the streets of Gotham safe.

programme.title: The Exorcist
programme.desc: A famous actress calls upon medical science for help when her daughter begins behaving strangely, but without any success. Then, as bizarre and horrific incidents involving the daughter increase, her mother turns in desperation to the Church, and to a priest who doubts his own faith.

programme.title: Waterworld
programme.desc: Big-budget tale of the battle for survival in a world where dry land has disappeared with the melting of the polar ice caps. One man, who has become amphibious, tries to protect survivors on an artificial atoll made from scrap metal by taking on a gang of marauding pirates. He is also intent on discovering if there is any truth in the myth that there is still some land left above water.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: The twins are asked to collect McCogginss bell for a town performance. Despite Battersbys warning that the clapper is fragile and doesnt have a spare, Tommy and Tallulah manage to break it. Deciding that they must make a replacement, they look everywhere for something to use and try some very unusual options. Will the bell be ready for Chime Time?

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: When Tommy and Tallulah agree to babysit the spring chicks while Madame Au Lait tends to Mother Chicks hurt wing, they dont realise that how much of a handful the chicks will be. The twins are also taking part in the town kite display today, but somehow the chicks end up surfing on their kite, high in the sky! The twins must stage an airborne rescue before Chime Time.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: Tommy and Tallulah are organising McCogginss pile of interesting junk when a vitally important spring goes missing. They cant chime the time without it! The twins chase the spring all over town as it bounces around causing chaos, eventually ending up attaching Pufferty to his kennel. Every time he tries to go somewhere, the spring pulls him back.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: McCoggins tells the twins not to stray far, but they forget to warn Pufferty when they try to teach him how to play hide and seek. There are not many places in Tickety Town big enough to hide a huge dog train, so Pufferty heads far off into the hills. Where can he be? The race is on to find him by Chime Time.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: Tommy and Tallulah find harvesting very slow when they volunteer to pick Lopsiloos vegetables. Surely there must be some way to speed it up? However, when they hit upon the idea of using Pufferty to pull all the vegetables up at once, they are shocked to find that Puffertys carriages unhook and run amok through Tickety Town.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: The twins are asked to deliver a precious package to Madame Au Lait and decide that a ride with Pufferty is safest way to transport it. However, when Hopparoo challenges the lovable dog train to a race, the package bounces out of the carriage and embarks upon a crazy journey round Tickety Town, with Tommy and Tallulah in hot pursuit.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: Tickety Town is covered in fallen leaves, so Tommy and Tallulah are using McCogginss new prototype street sweeper to clear them. However, in their eagerness to speed things up, they go against his instructions and press the turbo button. Suddenly, the sweeper goes haywire, rampaging through town and sucking up anything in its path, including Madame Au Laits clock key and very nearly Hopparoo too.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: The whole town gathers to take part in McCoggins treasure hunt. Whoever finds the most cogs will be the winner. Knowing that McCoggins hides the cogs in the same places every year, Tommy and Tallulah are cruising to victory, until they realise that a quick win will spoil everyones day, but when they borrow McCoggins special clockwork rollerskates to put the cogs back, the skates zoom out of control.
