programme.desc: n Southern Africa, Spring starts in early September and the natural world stirs into life. Days begin to lengthen, temperatures rise, and animals and plants combine in an orchestrated effort to reproduce, give birth, and raise their young.

programme.title: New Kids in the Wild
programme.desc: Follows Southern muriqui Dora after she takes her first steps away from her mom Mimi. Later, explores spider monkey troop dynamics and tail-hanging technique.

programme.title: Coral City
programme.desc: Discover the most fantastic city of them all lying beneath the waves, where romance and migratory commuters flood the coral reefs main highways. Follow a green sea turtle looking for love and sit in with a cassowary dad tending his newborn chicks.

programme.title: Into the Wild Colombia
programme.desc: Mona, a female cotton top tamarin is all alone in her tropical forest home. She has left the security of her birth family to search for an unrelated troop which might provide her with a future mate.

programme.title: Guardians of the Wild
programme.desc: Madonna is the first Kinda baboon ever to be studied from birth to adulthood and now, critically, she is looking for a mate. Only recently identified as a species in their own right, Kinda baboons are little known and little understood.

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: Beni, obsessed with food, shudders as he receives a dramatic, educational smackdown from the local dominant male, Petruk. Meanwhile, Spanser, a spoiled rich kid and former illegal pet, attempts to control his sweet tooth.

programme.title: New Kids in the Wild
programme.desc: An adventure with Thiago, a brave baby coati. Explores how this raccoon-like animal sharpens his sniffing and foraging skills, crosses suburban roads near his lush Brazilian forest home, and avoids inanimate and four-legged dangers.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: A look at the lives of monkeys, chimps, orangutans and apes at the worlds biggest sanctuary, Monkey World Ape Rescue Centre.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: A look at the lives of monkeys, chimps, orangutans and apes at the worlds biggest sanctuary, Monkey World Ape Rescue Centre.

programme.title: Guardians of the Wild
programme.desc: Across Africa 97 black rhinos have been killed for their horns. In 1997 the species was declared officially extinct in Zambia, but now, thanks to an ambitious reintroduction plan, they are back.

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: The race to contain a case of malaria brought in by orphaned infant Cesia is followed by an assessment of Sris plan to help Bumi. Meanwhile, observers notice the positive influence Daput, a wild orangutan mum, has on the college females.

programme.title: Into the Wild India
programme.desc: In the hot, and humid tropical forests of Southern India, a female King Cobra is on a mission. She needs to find a mate, find food and lay her eggs, all before the monsoon arrives in 5 months time.

programme.title: Africas Hunters
programme.desc: Two big male lions, twin brothers with a powerful bond, have ruled the Nsefu Pride for three years. Theyre fathers to all the young, and protectors of their territory.

programme.title: Deadly Australians
programme.desc: Behind the facade of Australias dramatic scenery is a motley crew of silent assassins, con artists, gang members and brutal brawlers, vying for supremacy amidst the harsh, dry and desolate Australian desert.

programme.desc: A look at the lives of monkeys, chimps, orangutans and apes at the worlds biggest sanctuary, Monkey World Ape Rescue Centre.

programme.title: Chasing the Rains
programme.desc: One of the most majestic, unspoiled and rarely filmed areas in Africa is an exploration of how wildlife including cheetahs, hyenas and elephants survive the cycles of draught and rainfall.

programme.title: Wolves and Lions: Old Enemies
programme.desc: Canids and felines deploy an arsenal of hunting skills. Felines face endurance, while wolves rely on their privileged sense of smell in a constant fight for survival.

programme.desc: Thieves target the Edinburgh to London mail train; the only clue Maguire can find is a footprint by the railway track.

programme.title: The Long and the Short and the Tall
programme.desc: A British sonic warfare patrol capture a Japanese scout in the Burmese jungle, but as they decide his fate they lose all contact with their commanding forces.

programme.title: Grey Cars: Devon 1957
programme.desc: A short film showing a beautiful trip across Devon in the 1950s.

programme.title: Those British Faces
programme.desc: 1993. Documentary series that acts as a tribute to the greats of British cinema. This episode focuses on a magnificent tribute to Miles Malleson.

programme.title: Out of Town
programme.desc: Sheep Dogs/Gun Collection. 1981. On a bitterly cold day, Jack Hargreaves travels to Wales for a sheep dog trial. Back in Hampshire he visits a collection of guns from the wild west of America.

programme.title: Scotland Yard
programme.desc: A payroll van is pulled over by a police car - the guards shot and the payroll stolen.

programme.title: Buccaneer
programme.desc: Tony lands in an African prison, Monica uncovers damaging information and Janet drops a financial bombshell.

programme.title: The Stranglers of Bombay
programme.desc: In the 1830s, a captain in the East India Company lobbies to investigate the criminal Thuggee cult of Kali, an organised crime group of stranglers and thieves who have been murdering travellers and stealing goods.

programme.title: The Challenge
programme.desc: Jailed for his role in a gang heist and ditched by its female leader, a widower decides to keep the loot.

programme.title: Budgie
programme.desc: Budgie always wanted to make money. When he meets Inky Ballantine he gets involved in a financial caper.

programme.title: Captain Apache
programme.desc: A full-blooded Apache Indian working for the US Army is assigned to investigate the murder of a colleague. When he uncovers a plot by a rich white farmer to instigate an Indian war in the course of his inquiries, the gunplay and bloodshed begin.

programme.title: False Colors
programme.desc: Hopalong Cassidy and his sidekicks expose a scheme to replace a ranch heir with an impostor.

programme.desc: Featuring a compilation of non-stop music from the 1960s.

programme.title: Lindbergh Baby Mystery
programme.desc: After Lindberghs fame, his baby was kidnapped in 1932. Carpenter Hauptmann was executed, but mysteries remain. Narrated by Robert Powell.

programme.title: Secrets of WW2
programme.desc: From the embarrassment of surrender, the French Resistance developed over the course of WWII into an effective and brave fighting force. They played a key role in the liberation of France.

programme.title: Gladiators of World War II
programme.desc: Elite combat units from the Second World War. The formidable Waffen-SS were renowned for their brutality and tenacity, characteristics which made them the subject of numerous Allied war crime investigations.

programme.title: Plane Resurrection
programme.desc: The series takes viewers into the cockpits of the worlds greatest restored fighting aircraft, and into the astonishing lives of the modern day heroes who are dedicating themselves to bringing these engineering masterpieces back to life.

programme.title: The Concise History of WWII
programme.desc: A look at the liberation of the concentration camps and the arrests and trials of the surviving Nazi leaders for war crimes. The end of the Second World War.

programme.title: Battlefield
programme.desc: Tim Piggott-Smith narrates the story of the Soviet victory over the mighty Japanese Kwangtung Army in the mountains of Manchuria, which is one of the least known of the second World War.

programme.title: Battleplan
programme.desc: This time looking at three different sieges: The first is the German siege of Leningrad from 1941-43, which Hitler simply wanted to starve into surrender, the second is the Viet Minh victory at Dien Bien Phu in 1953 and finally, the Viet Cong siege of the US base at Khe Sanh in 1968, when US commanders drew their enemy into an exhausting confrontation.

programme.title: Naval Aviation in WW2
programme.desc: A look at the role of naval crafts and aircraft in WW2 with eyewitness accounts about how campaigns were planned and what happened on the day of attack when protection from the enemy was very limited.

programme.title: 21st Century War Machines
programme.sub-title: Heavy Metal: Tank vs. Anti Tank
programme.desc: 20th century warfare has the battle tanks tread marks all over it, but it might not translate to the 21st Century too well. Its effectiveness, cost, and differences to the modern battle tank is examined.

programme.title: Desert Generals
programme.desc: This is the story of the forgotten Battle for Egypt and the inside picture of the tactics that stopped the German tanks in their tracks.

programme.desc: The tank which first reared its head in World War I, fine-tuned by the Nazis as an integral part of their blitzkrieg tactics. As their Panzers conquered most of Europe, the Soviets were developing their own T34, more suited to their own harsher terrain, a turning point in the war.

programme.title: Battleplan

programme.desc: Rob Brydon is in the hosts chair for the award-winning comedy panel show, with team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell. programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The chance discovery of a camera and rolls of film hidden in a pub toilet leads the team to reopen the case of a young man murdered whilst working in a photography shop. programme.desc: Gloria, a friend of Nora Batty, is emigrating to Australia. Its up to the trio to find a piano for her surprise farewell party, but will Blamire impress everyone with his ivory-tinkling?

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The trio thinks they have too much time on their hands, and in trying to find some kind of occupation, they get caught up in a disastrous scheme.

programme.desc: Compo finally gets a chance to seduce Nora Batty when Wally Batty threatens to leave home. Compo does everything he can think of to impress her, like cleaning his home and dressing smartly.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek navigates the dry stone walls of the Peak District to collect a very original 1988 Opel Manta, Dave handles a rare Ford Granada rally support vehicle, and Paul contends with a Hilux monster truck.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek takes a long drive in a 1960s bus that has had a life in the sun, Dave helps a collector trim his fleet of British classics by taking a Triumph TR7 convertible for auction, and Paul is confused by an agricultural Bonser truck.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek gets a tour of Hadlow Castle in Kent when he collects an immaculate Citroen that was once used by the Gestapo in occupied France, Dave considers spending his own money on a hay cart, and Paul unearths a remarkably rust-free 1970s Alfa GTV.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: The Mathewson family continue their pursuit of classic cars. In the home counties, Derek picks up two expertly crafted replicas of the batmobile. Meanwhile, Dave struggles to contain his enthusiasm for a Second World War Ford jeep.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Paul picks up a stunning 1961 Vauxhall Cresta, Dave drools over a Ford Sierra Sapphire Cosworth, and Derek refuses a test drive in the worlds smallest production car, the 3.5 horsepower Peel P50.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek inspects a collection of old police cars that includes a Rover P6 complete with radio, sirens and V8 engine. Meanwhile, Paul tries to figure out who will buy an old 1975 Range Rover that has been converted into a fire engine.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek swaps the chaos of the office for rain-soaked Ripon to collect a 1965 Ford Anglia with just 33,000 miles on the clock. Meanwhile, Dave enjoys the clean lines of a rare Renault Caravalle.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Paul sets out for County Durham in order to collect both a 1936 Singer Le Mans sports car and a pristine 1957 Lambretta LD 150. In the meantime, Derek gets his hands on a 1962 MGA MK2 Coupe.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek is reunited with the vehicle he took his driving test in, a 1957 Ford 100E Estate. Meanwhile, Paul heads north to collect a 50-year-old MGBGT that still smells like a new car.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Homeowners across Britain try to complete do-it-yourself home projects on their own. But sometimes, they need help finishing the work. Thats where presenter Nick Knowles and his team of designers and builders step in. They help homeowners complete their DIY projects gone awry.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Homeowners across Britain try to complete do-it-yourself home projects on their own. But sometimes, they need help finishing the work. Thats where presenter Nick Knowles and his team of designers and builders step in. They help homeowners complete their DIY projects gone awry.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: The DIY SOS teams is back and in Birmingham helping a family in need. Charlotte and Chris have twins who were born prematurely and have a condition known as global development delay. Their cramped house is hindering their progress even more and if they have any chance of catching up, theye going to need DIY SOS and a host of volunteers from the local community to rebuild their lives and give the family a better future.

programme.desc: A buyer looking for a fresh start hunts for a modern turnkey property to be her new home base. The search is on for a two-story home in the same community but Jonathan and Drew convince her to make a move she never saw coming.

programme.title: My Dream Home
programme.desc: Drew and Jonathan Scott help to rejuvenate and refresh Dereck and Christines house hunt.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The start of the new school term leads of a spate of stabbings and a forester is badly injured in an accident involving a chainsaw deep in woods near Keighley.

programme.title: Annika
programme.desc: On her first day in the new role, Annika (Nicola Walker) and the team are called in to solve the murder of a man found in the Clyde, a harpoon piercing his head.

programme.title: Annika
programme.desc: When a body is found on a boat in the Clyde, the case takes the team to the Isle of Bute. Evidence points to a jilted lover, but as the team investigate, a darker secret starts to emerge.

programme.title: Tortured to Death: Murdering the Nanny
programme.desc: An investigation into the murder of Sophie Lionnet, a nanny whose burned body was found in the garden of her employers South London home.

programme.title: Katie Piper: Locked Up in Louisiana
programme.desc: Katie meets Tonica, who is accused of killing her husband, and learns that she suffered a lifetime of abuse both as a child and then at the hands of her husband.

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: The Death Cleaners help Suzi, a sassy 75-year-old woman surrounded by phallic souvenirs from her travels and a lifetime of photos from her years as a singing waitress, let go of the past and remind her that shes not dead yet.

programme.title: Deepfake Porn: You Could Be Next
programme.desc: Most people think of deepfakes as manipulated video clips of world leaders, designed to create confusion, but the most pressing threat of deepfakes is not politics, but porn. Research shows around 96 per cent of deepfake videos are pornographic, with almost 100 per cent of them involving non-consenting women. The film features three women who have been impacted by this form of image-based sexual abuse.

programme.desc: Contestants play a large coin-drop machine, provided they answer questions correctly. Answer incorrectly and the players could be eliminated. The last contestant standing has an opportunity to win the machines £10,000 jackpot.

programme.title: My Dream Home
programme.desc: Together with their toddler and a newborn, Denise and Massimo are squeezed into his old bachelor pad condo. But theyre more than ready to settle down in a dream home.

programme.title: My Dream Home
programme.desc: Jonathan tries to help Jana and Larry, a couple who were in the middle of renovating their dream home when a family crisis moved them back in with parents.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Homeowners across Britain try to complete do-it-yourself home projects on their own. But sometimes, they need help finishing the work. Thats where presenter Nick Knowles and his team of designers and builders step in. They help homeowners complete their DIY projects gone awry.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Homeowners across Britain try to complete do-it-yourself home projects on their own. But sometimes, they need help finishing the work. Thats where presenter Nick Knowles and his team of designers and builders step in. They help homeowners complete their DIY projects gone awry.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs Sake
programme.desc: Pete travels to Northern Ireland to pick up three stray dogs and bring them back with him to Essex. He also meets a Shar Pei who is in constant pain and may need surgery.

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: Midwife Linda Abbott returns to Leeds General. A year after retiring she realised she missed working at the hospital. Two dads with extremely different bedside manners are in the maternity wards in this episode. Joel dotes on his wife Rachel and wants there just to be the two of them in the delivery room. Dan, meanwhile, prefers digs towards his partner Kerry.

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: Theres a nationwide baby-boom in September and the midwives at the Leeds General Infirmary are run off their feet. When their unit is full they often send the overspill to St Jamess Hospital across the city and vice versa, which means a last-minute change in birth plans for some mums, including Natalie, Kate, and Rachel.

programme.title: Emergency: First Time Medics
programme.desc: Its induction day for 26 junior doctors as they pick up their passes and scrubs for the Emergency Department and try to get their bearings around the Royal Berkshire Hospital.

programme.title: Emergency: First Time Medics
programme.desc: Junior doctor Alex handles a paracetamol overdose and a high-risk heart patient, Adnan braves his first shift in Resus, and Claire deals with a woman in agonising stomach pain.

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: Shana knows she can be uptight, but talking to friends about a terminal cancer diagnosis is daunting; the Death Cleaners help her face her fear of discussing "the end."

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews...
programme.desc: Louis spends time with platinum-selling rapper Stormzy. After two years out of the spotlight, Stormzy welcomes Louis into his home in south west London to sit down for a frank and revealing conversation that covers many aspects of his life, from his childhood to the breakdown of a high-profile relationship, as well as the pressures that come with being one of the countrys best-loved musicians and role models.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in East Anglia on the last leg of his rail journey through 1930s Britain. He begins in Attleborough in Norfolk at the headquarters of an international horse welfare organisation which was established in the late 1920s and learns about the charitys pioneering founder. Crossing the Fens, Michaels next stop is the cathedral city of Peterborough.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo strikes out on another series of railway journeys, this time through Britain between the world wars, armed with his 1930s Bradshaws guide. He begins with a tour of the Home Counties and beyond.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his travels through Englands Home Counties at Stoke Mandeville in Buckinghamshire, where he discovers the legacy of a German Jewish doctor who fled the Nazis to settle in Britain.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads for leafy Hatch End, where he investigates an illustrator whose work was so popular in the 1920s and 30s that his name entered the English dictionary. William Heath Robinsons great-nephew talks Michael through some of the mad contraptions the artist used to satirise technology.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave has to extricate a VW Golf from a garden that has grown up around it.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek is off to Bridlington to pick-up a trio of classics: a Morris 8 tourer, a Mini Van and a 1940s Morris LC3 truck.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: Stricken by the humiliation of defeat and the harsh terms of the Treaty of Versailles, Germany looks to one man for a resurgence of hope and dignity - Adolf Hitler.

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: Crossword puzzles in a national newspaper begin to attract the attention of MI5, as the answers are code words for the upcoming D-Day operation.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Antiques Roadshow returns to one of the smallest cities in England - Wells in Somerset - where the Bishops Palace hosts Fiona and the team. Elaine Binning hears the story of a gymnast who competed in the 1928 Olympics, the first time the games admitted female gymnastic competitors.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return to Ham House and gardens in Richmond-upon-Thames. Treasures are in plentiful supply, with miscellaneous expert Bunny Campione valuing an 18th-century effigy and military expert Mark Smith coming across an Indian Order of Merit medal awarded to a Sikh solder in the First World War. Other items include an Omega wristwatch, an 18th-century pistol and a gold necklace from the Alaskan Gold Rush.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: A super rare Reliant Scimitar GTC undergoes an expert recommission, leaving no bolt unturned. And a Honda CBR superbike from the 80s is in need of repair.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team are on the hunt to restore one of the greatest names in motoring history - the Bugatti. But with a strict budget they settle for a replica.

programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon Reeve travels from the islands of the Aegean to Greeces capital, Athens. Getting behind the postcard image of this beautiful country, he finds out how the Greeks are coming to terms with a seemingly endless crisis.

programme.desc: The adventures of a family run crew of heavy-haulage specialists as they race to transport the heaviest, longest and most precious locomotives around the world by road, rail and sea. Plus, footage reveals the history behind the featured locomotives.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo resumes his tour of the Home Counties in the picturesque and historic county town of Guildford. His 1936 Bradshaws guide commends its `fine old castle keep and guildhall but makes no mention of its hilltop cathedral. Michael meets head guide Janet Matthews to discover the origins of the first Anglican cathedral to be built on a new site in the south of England since the Reformation, begun in 1936.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael is heading for Farnborough, Hampshire, famous today for its airshow and home to what was then the Royal Aircraft Establishment. Richard Gardner of the Farnborough Air Sciences Trust tells Michael about the boffins who worked here and the inventions they came up with, such as retractable landing gear and flight-pressurised suits.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his 1930s Bradshaws guide, Michael Portillo explores East Anglia between the wars. His railway journey begins at Sutton Hoo in Suffolk, where archaeologists made a staggering discovery.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his Bradshaws-inspired journey through East Anglia, where he discovers the Essex origins of the BBC, joins the Womens Land Army to pick damsons at Tiptree, and visits homes fit for heroes in Becontree.

programme.desc: Fiona and the team return to the Ulster Folk Museum in Northern Ireland, where items include a DeLorean car, a stone head dug up in a garden and a carving with links to maritime history.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Silver expert Kate Flitcroft is shaken, not stirred, by a stunning fire extinguisher cocktail shaker that turns up, while ceramics expert Eric Knowles meets his match with a cheeky toy monkey popping out of a musical pineapple.
